title: Blocpod Riverpod Event Architecture Design
description: Pit Wall의 Riverpod event-controller 패턴을 재사용 가능한 Blocpod 패키지 워크스페이스로 추출하는 설계 계약입니다.

page.humanSummary: Blocpod는 Riverpod 위에 BLoC식 typed event dispatch, trace, logging 경계를 더하는 얇은 아키텍처 계층입니다.
page.agentSummary: Blocpod 패키지 의존성 방향, public API, dispatch/logging 계약을 변경하기 전에 이 JSON과 원본 Superpowers spec을 먼저 확인하세요.

body.goal: Pit Wall의 Riverpod event-controller 패턴을 `blocpod_arch`, `blocpod_logger`, `blocpod_arch_logger` 패키지로 분리해 재사용 가능한 Dart/Flutter 아키텍처 라이브러리로 만든다.
body.problem: 현재 패턴은 애플리케이션 내부 구현에 묶여 있어 다른 Riverpod 기반 앱에서 typed event dispatch, clean-architecture boundary, event traceability, structured logging을 일관되게 재사용하기 어렵다.

in: `packages/logger`의 generic log sink와 log entry primitives
`packages/arch_logger`의 architecture event record to log entry bridge
dispatch lifecycle, trace context, event log record, no-op default logger
out: OpenTelemetry, Sentry, Crashlytics 같은 외부 logger adapter 초도 구현
domain params를 통한 trace id 전달

architecture.summary: `blocpod_arch`는 Riverpod event architecture의 안정 계약을 소유하고 concrete logger를 알지 않는다. `blocpod_logger`는 architecture primitive를 모르는 generic output sink를 소유한다. `blocpod_arch_logger`만 두 패키지를 함께 알아 `EventLogRecord`를 `BlocpodLogEntry`로 변환한다.
flow: `EventControllerNotifier.dispatch()` creates or enters `TraceContext`, captures before state, and records start time.
Use case returns `Result<T>` and repository/data source maps expected failures.
Dispatch captures after state and emits `EventLogRecord` through `EventLogger`.

requirements.title: Riverpod 위의 BLoC식 event boundary
requirements.detail: 모든 user/system action은 controller public method가 아니라 `dispatch(E event)` 경계로 들어와야 한다.
acceptanceCriteria: event-specific handler는 controller 내부의 protected/private 흐름에 머문다.
widget과 provider code가 `ref.dispatch(provider, event)`를 사용할 수 있다.

requirements.title: Clean architecture primitive
requirements.detail: expected domain/data failure는 `Result<T>`로 표현하고 use case는 `UseCase<Output, Params>` boundary를 따른다.
acceptanceCriteria: recoverable user-facing issue는 state field에 남기고 unrecoverable screen failure만 `AsyncError`로 전환한다.

requirements.title: Trace context and structured event record
requirements.detail: 각 dispatch는 root 또는 child span을 가지며 before/after state kind, duration, error, sanitized metadata를 payload-free record로 남긴다.
acceptanceCriteria: nested dispatch는 같은 trace id와 child span을 가진다.
`EventLogRecord`는 full state payload나 secret을 기본으로 담지 않는다.

requirements.title: Logger decoupling
requirements.detail: core architecture는 concrete logger sink를 import하지 않고 no-op default로 설치 가능해야 한다.
acceptanceCriteria: app은 provider override로 `BlocpodEventLogger(DebugPrintLogSink())` 같은 adapter를 설치한다.

requirements.title: Package dependency direction
requirements.detail: 패키지 dependency graph는 one-way로 고정하고 cyclic dependency를 만들지 않는다.
acceptanceCriteria: `blocpod_logger` depends on no Blocpod architecture package.
dependency direction tests and manual `rg` checks prove forbidden imports are absent.

approaches.name: Recommended: three-package split
tradeoffs: core architecture and output formatting stay decoupled.
bridge package adds one more package but keeps dependency direction explicit.
apps opt into logging without changing controller code.

approaches.name: Rejected: core package imports logger sink directly
tradeoffs: architecture package becomes coupled to output concerns.
future external sink adapters would pressure core dependencies.

approaches.name: Rejected: generated controller classes
tradeoffs: Riverpod generator workflow와 맞아 보일 수 있다.
현재 architecture baseline의 explicit `AsyncNotifierProvider` and `EventControllerNotifier<S, E>` contract와 충돌한다.
approaches.recommendation: 초도 Blocpod architecture에서는 generated `@riverpod` controller class를 만들지 않는다.

components.name: blocpod_arch
components.responsibility: core event architecture, dispatch lifecycle, `Result`, `UseCase`, trace context, event records, logger interface, no-op logger provider를 소유한다.

components.name: blocpod_logger
components.responsibility: generic logging primitives와 local-development debug output formatting을 제공하되 Riverpod architecture primitive를 import하지 않는다.

dataFlow: dispatch -> before `AsyncValue` capture and start time record

errorHandling.behavior: 화면 state field에 남기고 architecture-level error로 승격하지 않는다.
`dispatch`가 original stack trace와 함께 `EventLogRecord`에 기록하고 호출자에게 rethrow한다.
application flow를 깨지 않도록 logger implementation이 sink error를 catch and isolate한다.
records and formatted logs must not include secrets, credentials, tokens, passwords, or full raw payloads by default.

contracts.name: Event dispatch boundary
rules: Every user or system action enters through `dispatch`.
Controllers expose only `dispatch` as their public action API.
Event-specific handlers stay private/protected inside the controller.

contracts.name: Trace context
rules: Create a root `TraceContext` when dispatch starts and no current trace exists.
Nested dispatches reuse current trace id and create child spans.
Application code must not pass `traceId` through use case parameters.
Trace context is observability concern, not a domain model.

contracts.name: Event log record
rules: Record state kind and transition shape, not full state object payloads.
Metadata must be sanitized and feature-supplied.
Record controller name, event name, duration, trace, state transition, error, stack trace.

contracts.name: Dependency direction
rules: Domain use cases must not depend on logger adapters.
Feature state must not contain trace context.

surfaces.role: Public barrel for core architecture APIs.
Core Result, UseCase, dispatch, trace, log record, logger provider implementation.
Public barrel for generic logger APIs.
Generic log level, entry, sink, debug print formatter.
Public barrel for architecture logger bridge APIs.
Event record formatter and `EventLogger` adapter implementation.

testingStrategy.expected: `Result<T>`, `UseCase`, dispatch routing, provider variants, trace/logging, default no-op logger, thrown error preservation, dependency direction tests pass.
`BlocpodLogEntry`, debug print formatting, sensitive metadata redaction, and no-import-from-arch guard pass.
`BlocpodEventLogger` maps records, error records become error-level entries, and sink failure isolation passes.

testingStrategy.command: rg -n "package:blocpod_logger/" packages/arch/lib packages/arch/test && rg -n "package:blocpod_arch/" packages/logger/lib packages/logger/test
testingStrategy.expected: forbidden dependency searches print no matches.

validation.purpose: Stenc spec JSON schema and required fields are valid.
Regenerate the fixed-format Stenc web pages from JSON source.
Confirm this JSON document has a matching rendered web page.

agentInstructions: Before changing Blocpod architecture APIs, read this Stenc JSON and the linked Superpowers spec.
Keep `blocpod_arch` independent from concrete logger packages.
Keep trace context out of domain params and feature state.
Do not add generated `@riverpod` controller classes to this architecture without a new decision record.
When updating this contract, update the related Stenc plan and regenerate `docs/stenc`.
reviewChecklist: Architecture and logging output remain decoupled.
Package dependency graph has no cycles.
Event-controller API remains simple for feature authors.
Traceability does not leak into domain models.
Implementation plan can be executed incrementally without changing Pit Wall behavior first.

selfReviewChecks.name: Completeness
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Requirements, architecture, components, data flow, error handling, dependency direction, and testing strategy are represented in native Stenc fields.

selfReviewChecks.name: Source fidelity
selfReviewChecks.purpose: The Stenc contract preserves the Superpowers spec meaning while linking the original Markdown as source evidence.

selfReviewChecks.name: Agent safety
selfReviewChecks.purpose: Agent instructions identify forbidden imports, trace leakage, generated-controller drift, and rendering regeneration requirements.

notes: Preferred execution route is `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` for one task per worker.
Inline execution may use `superpowers:executing-plans` with review checkpoints.
The original Markdown plan remains the detailed code-block source of truth.

supportingSections.heading: Product Positioning
supportingSections.content: Blocpod는 Riverpod replacement가 아니라 Riverpod provider model, `AsyncNotifier`, dependency injection, testing ergonomics를 유지하면서 BLoC-style traceability를 더하는 얇은 event architecture layer다.
items: Name signals BLoC-like Riverpod, not a full BLoC clone.
Published package names carry the `blocpod_` prefix.
Local folder names stay short for workspace ergonomics.

supportingSections.heading: Pit Wall Migration Outline
supportingSections.content: Pit Wall migration is downstream work after Blocpod package tests and docs pass.
items: Add logger primitives and bridge adapter.
Update Pit Wall to depend on `blocpod_arch` by local path.
Replace local core architecture imports with `package:blocpod_arch/blocpod_arch.dart`.
Remove duplicated local core architecture only after all app and package tests pass.
Align English and Korean architecture docs plus conventions.

supportingSections.heading: Milestones
supportingSections.content: Implementation can proceed incrementally without requiring Pit Wall behavior changes first.
items: Milestone 1: Extract core architecture primitives and no-op event logging.
Milestone 3: Add logger packages and adapter mapping tests.
Milestone 4: Update Pit Wall documentation to point to Blocpod packages.
